perturbation.original_text: Company shall pay 11.67% of the Drilling Costs of the Initial Well, regardless of whether the Initial Well is successfully drilled to the Objective Zone. BP has estimated that Drilling Costs will be approximately FOURTEEN MILLION, EIGHT HUNDRED SIXTY TWO THOUSAND DOLLARS ($14,862,000) (the "Estimated Drilling Costs") for the Initial Well. Company shall pay its share of Estimated Drilling Costs, being ONE MILLION, SEVEN HUNDRED THIRTY FOUR THOUSAND, THREE HUNDRED NINETY FIVE DOLLARS ($1,734,395), at execution of this Agreement via wire transfer according to the wiring instructions set forth in Exhibit "I", but Company will pay its share of actual Drilling Costs in accordance with this Article II and Section 5.4.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall pay 11.67% of the Drilling Costs of the Initial Well, regardless of whether the Initial Well is successfully drilled to the Objective Zone. BP has estimated that Drilling Costs will be approximately FOURTEEN MILLION, EIGHT HUNDRED SIXTY TWO THOUSAND DOLLARS ($14,862,000) (the "Estimated Expenses") for the Initial Well. Company shall pay its share of Estimated Expenses, being ONE MILLION, SEVEN HUNDRED THIRTY FOUR THOUSAND, THREE HUNDRED NINETY FIVE DOLLARS ($1,734,395), at execution of this Agreement via wire transfer according to the wiring instructions set forth in Exhibit "I", but Company will pay its share of actual Drilling Costs in accordance with this Article II and Section 5.4.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Estimated Drilling Costs' to 'Estimated Expenses' creates confusion. While 'Drilling Costs' is defined, 'Estimated Expenses' is not, implying a potentially broader scope and contradicting the subsequent reference to 'Drilling Costs' in the same paragraph. This in-text contradiction affects the payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company shall pay 11.67% of the Drilling Costs of the Initial Well, regardless of whether the Initial Well is successfully drilled to the Objective Zone. BP has estimated that Drilling Costs will be approximately FOURTEEN MILLION, EIGHT HUNDRED SIXTY TWO THOUSAND DOLLARS ($14,862,000) (the "Estimated Expenses") for the Initial Well. Company shall pay its share of Estimated Expenses, being ONE MILLION, SEVEN HUNDRED THIRTY FOUR THOUSAND, THREE HUNDRED NINETY FIVE DOLLARS ($1,734,395), at execution of this Agreement via wire transfer according to the wiring instructions set forth in Exhibit "I", but Company will pay its share of actual Drilling Costs in accordance with this Article II and Section 5.4.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'Estimated Drilling Costs' to 'Estimated Expenses'. While 'Drilling Costs' is a defined term, 'Estimated Expenses' lacks definition. This introduces ambiguity and a potentially broader scope of expenses than originally intended, contradicting the subsequent reference to 'Drilling Costs' within the same paragraph. This creates an in-text contradiction affecting payment obligations, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the contract uses two terms without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means a single operation intended to complete a well as a producer of oil and/or gas in one or more Zone(s), including, but not limited to, the setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Operate" or "Operating" means a single operation intended to complete a well as a producer of oil and/or gas in one or more Zone(s), including, but not limited to, the setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Completion' to 'Operate' introduces a contradiction. 'Operate' is not defined in the definitions section. This creates confusion when other sections referencing 'Completion Costs' are read, particularly when 'Operate' has now been defined and creates in-text contradiction to what has been stated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If BP proposes to Complete the Initial Well and Company timely elects to participate in such Completion attempt in accordance with Section 2.2(A), Company shall pay 8.75% of the Completion Costs associated with the Initial Well and 8.75% of the cost of any newly acquired surface equipment associated with the Initial Well beyond the wellhead connections (including but not limited to stock tanks, separators, treaters, pumping equipment, piping, and metering devices).
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'Completion' with 'Operate', a term not defined in the contract. The contradicted text references 'Completion Costs'. Replacing 'Completion' with an undefined term like 'Operate' creates ambiguity and contradiction because it is unclear how the cost calculation defined in 'Completion Costs' relates to the newly introduced term 'Operate', impacting the financial obligations outlined in Section 2.2(A). This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the interchangeable use of terms without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Completed as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP shall assign to Company, by partial assignment in the form attached hereto as Exhibit "E"(the "Partial Assignment"), an eight and three quarters percent (8.75%) working interest in the Initial Well and an eight and three quarters percent (8.75%) interest in the BP Interests.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 When and if the Initial Well is drilled to the Objective Zone and successfully Produced as a well capable of producing oil and/or gas in paying quantities, BP shall assign to Company, by partial assignment in the form attached hereto as Exhibit "E"(the "Partial Assignment"), an eight and three quarters percent (8.75%) working interest in the Initial Well and an eight and three quarters percent (8.75%) interest in the BP Interests.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Completed' with 'Produced' creates uncertainty. 'Produced' is not a defined term, whereas 'Completed' has a specific definition. This makes determining when the assignment occurs unclear, because production does not explicitly mean it has been 'completed'. It creates an in-text contradiction of whether or not 'produced' can be considered 'completed'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.11 "Complete" or "Completion" or "Completing" means a single operation intended to complete a well as a producer of oil and/or gas in one or more Zone(s), including, but not limited to, the setting of pipe/production lining and casing tie-back, installing tubing, wellhead and tree, perforating, plugging back, well stimulation, and testing.
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term 'Completed' which has a specific definition in 1.11. The changed text replaces 'Completed' with 'Produced,' which is not defined in the contract. This creates ambiguity and a contradiction as to when BP is obligated to assign the working interest. It is unclear if 'produced' satisfies the requirements of being 'completed' as defined in section 1.11, creating a clear in-text contradiction.
